name: Herbed Corn on the Cob
recipeIngredient: 1/4 cup Butter, softened to room temperature
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 375°F. Cut out four large squares of aluminum foil.
2. Peel back (but do not remove) the corn husks. Remove the corn silk.
3. In a small bowl, blend together the butter, basil, oregano, salt, and pepper.
4. Spread about 1 tablespoon of the herbed butter on each ear of corn. Close the husk around the corn.
5. Wrap each ear of corn tightly in foil and bake for 30 minutes or until tender.
Time-Savers:
MICROWAVE TIP: To cook the corn in the microwave, after spreading the butter on (step 4), sprinkle a few drops of water over the kernels. Then tightly wrap in plastic wrap. Cook (on paper towels if any butter leaks out) for 12 to 15 minutes on HIGH, rearranging the corn halfway through cooking. Let stand 5 minutes and serve.
DO-AHEAD: The herbed butter for the corn can be made well ahead of time and stored in the refrigerator or freezer. You can even double or triple the quantities and keep it on hand, frozen for any time you have corn on the cob.
